extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMAN Pascal won the sprint in Tour of Guangxi Stage 2, narrowly defeating JAKOBSEN Fabio after Bora-Hansgrohe controlled the break
tactical_patterns: Bora-Hansgrohe controlled breakaway without committing riders, textbook lead-out execution suggests superior sprint train organization versus Deceuninck.
GRIVKO Andriy in break two consecutive days signals Mitchelton positioning for intermediate sprints rather than sprint train support.
VANYA Rémi contested all intermediate sprints despite being in break, revealing Deceuninck prioritizing bonus seconds over stage win.

rider_signals.ACKERMAN Pascal: positive: fourth WorldTour win of 2018 demonstrates legitimate top-tier sprint threat, not just national circuit specialist
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: vanquished in sprint while wearing red jersey suggests either tactical miscalculation or inferior lead-out compared to Bora
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: second place without teammate support from break shows individual positioning ability in chaotic finishes
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: DNS on Stage 2 signals BMC dysfunction in team's final season, abandoning before any decisive terrain
